item.title: Backup: unencrypted archive created without mode 0600 and snapsDir missing chmodSync — transient exposure and existing-install gap
item.intent: The unencrypted backup archive must be created with mode 0600 to eliminate world-readable exposure during the encryption window, and chmodSync must be added after mkdirSync so existing installs with 0755 snapshots directories are fixed.
item.description: src/operate/backup/backup.ts has two permission gaps: (1) line 210 calls createWriteStream(archivePath) without a mode option, creating the unencrypted tar.gz with default permissions (0644 — world-readable). This file exists from archive creation through GPG encryption (Step 2) and is deleted in Step 5 Cleanup. On a multi-user system, another user can read the unencrypted archive during this window. (2) line 166 calls mkdirSync(snapsDir, { recursive: true, mode: DIR_MODE_SECRET }) without a following chmodSync, so on existing installs where snapshots/ was previously created at 0755, the mode is never corrected. GPG-encrypted backup files (.tar.gz.gpg) and manifest files are created by external processes or writeFile without explicit mode. Fix: (1) change createWriteStream(archivePath) to createWriteStream(archivePath, { mode: FILE_MODE_SECRET }) and add chmodSync(archivePath, FILE_MODE_SECRET) after the pipeline completes. (2) Add chmodSync(snapsDir, DIR_MODE_SECRET) after mkdirSync. Also add explicit mode to writeFile(manifestPath) for belt-and-suspenders.
steps: Read src/operate/backup/backup.ts lines 1-30 — confirm chmodSync is NOT in the node:fs import, and FILE_MODE_SECRET is NOT imported
Read src/operate/backup/backup.ts lines 155-175 — confirm mkdirSync(snapsDir, { recursive: true, mode: DIR_MODE_SECRET }) exists but has NO chmodSync call after it
Read src/operate/backup/backup.ts lines 205-215 — confirm createWriteStream(archivePath) has no mode option
Read src/operate/backup/backup.ts lines 245-255 — confirm writeFile(manifestPath, ...) has no mode option
Add chmodSync to the node:fs import in backup.ts
Add FILE_MODE_SECRET to the existing import from ../../config/defaults.js in backup.ts (DIR_MODE_SECRET is already imported)
Add chmodSync(snapsDir, DIR_MODE_SECRET) on the line immediately after mkdirSync(snapsDir, ...) — fixes existing installs where snapshots/ was created at 0755
Change createWriteStream(archivePath) to createWriteStream(archivePath, { mode: FILE_MODE_SECRET }) — restricts the unencrypted archive from creation time
After the await pipeline(...) line that writes the archive, add chmodSync(archivePath, FILE_MODE_SECRET) — enforces mode even if umask overrides
Change writeFile(manifestPath, ..., 'utf-8') to writeFile(manifestPath, ..., { encoding: 'utf-8', mode: FILE_MODE_SECRET }) — manifest contains backup metadata
Run the full test suite — confirm all backup tests pass with no regressions
acceptanceCriteria: The snapshots/ directory is created/fixed to mode 0700 — stat confirms no group/other read bits on existing installs
The unencrypted archive (.tar.gz) is created with mode 0600 — stat on a test run confirms no group/other read bits
The backup manifest (.manifest.json) is written with mode 0600
